commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat at highest level in 2 years. Black Sea and Persian Gulf shipping routes disrupted. Hormuz closure blocking fertilizer shipments at peak planting season.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Fertilizer supply chain from Middle East severely disrupted. Diesel costs for farming operations surging.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans continuing to climb. Shipping insurance costs quadrupled through Strait of Hormuz. Asian import routes rerouting via Cape of Good Hope.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Energy costs for refineries surging globally. Transport costs up 40% due to diesel price spikes.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Palm oil jumped 10% — shipping through Strait of Hormuz blocked. Alternative routing adds 12+ days and $933,000 per voyage.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately 33% of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea spiked from $450 to $600/tonne in one week. Worst timing: start of Northern Hemisphere planting season. UN warns of cascading food price impacts.

context: Food prices rising as oil crosses $100. Asian LNG spot prices doubled to $25.40/MMBtu after QatarEnergy declared force majeure at Ras Laffan. Fertilizer prices surging due to natural gas price spikes. Container shipping surcharges of $3,000+ in effect. Iraq oil production collapsed 70% from southern oilfields.
